stimulus.content: A geography class is studying three cities. City A is near the equator. City B is halfway between the equator and the North Pole. City C is near the North Pole.

stem: Based on their locations, which city most likely has the warmest average temperature all year?

choices.text: City A, because it is closest to the equator
City B, because it is in the middle latitude
City C, because it receives the most sunlight
City B, because middle zones trap the most heat

answer_explanation: Places near the equator receive the most direct sunlight year-round, giving them the warmest average temperatures. City C near the pole would be the coldest.
